response: I understand you've lost access to your Claude team workspace after your seat was removed. Unfortunately, the documentation states that for self-serve Enterprise plans, seats cannot be removed during the annual term, and reductions only take effect at renewal. If you need to make an exception, you would need to contact the Sales team to discuss converting to a sales-assisted plan. (claude/team-and-enterprise-plans/admin-management/13393991-purchase-and-manage-seats-on-enterprise-plans.md)
justification: The user is requesting to restore access to a Claude workspace after their seat was removed. The documentation indicates that seat removals on self-serve Enterprise plans are not immediate and require contacting sales for exceptions.

response:
I understand you're concerned about your test score and its impact on your application. While I cannot directly review your answers, increase your score, or instruct a company to move you to the next round, I can provide some information on how HackerRank test results are synced with Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS).

For systems like Workday, Oracle Recruiting Cloud, and Jobvite, test results, including scores and status updates, are automatically synced from HackerRank. If a score is changed in HackerRank after the initial sync, it can be updated in Workday and Jobvite. However, Greenhouse does not reflect updated scores after the initial sync (Source: hackerrank/integrations/applicant-tracking-systems/workday/1999755553-workday---hackerrank-tests-user-guide.md, hackerrank/integrations/applicant-tracking-systems/jobvite/2429924508-jobvite---hackerrank-integration-user-guide.md, hackerrank/integrations/applicant-tracking-systems/greenhouse/3678461564-greenhouse-hackerrank-integration-faqs.md).

To review your detailed report, you can log in to HackerRank for Work and search for your email address or navigate to the specific test and candidate tab. This will allow you to view your full test performance report (Source: hackerrank/integrations/applicant-tracking-systems/greenhouse/3678461564-greenhouse-hackerrank-integration-faqs.md).
justification: The user is disputing a test score and requesting intervention. The documentation explains how scores are synced to ATS and how users can view their own reports, but does not provide a mechanism for score disputes or direct intervention by HackerRank support.

response: If your payment fails, please refresh the page and retry the payment. If an amount was deducted incorrectly, it will be refunded within 5–10 business days. (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/subscriptions-payments-and-billing/9157064719-payments-and-billing-faqs.md). If you accidentally made a purchase or are not satisfied with your mock interview, please contact help@hackerrank.com and the support team will promptly review your request. (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/subscriptions-payments-and-billing/3282259518-purchase-mock-interviews.md)
justification: The user is asking for help with a payment issue. The documentation provides steps for failed payments and a contact email for issues with purchases.
